appraisal_events.evidence: Il assimile ses "garden-parties" à "des invites à se promener dans les égouts", parle d'"on a crevé ma fosse d’aisances" quand elle ouvre la bouche, puis, quand elle s'excuse, "ne daigna répondre autrement que par un large rire ironique" et un tardif « bonsoir ».
appraisal_events.explanation: Charlus publicly orchestrates an insulting tirade against the marquise and repels her even when she attempts a conciliation, excluding and debasing her. The narrator calls this speech abominable, marking an ironic distancing.

status_effects.explanation: Despite the narrator’s disapproval, Charlus dominates the situation through his aggressive wit and imposes his power of exclusion, which places him locally in a position of oratorical ascendancy.
She undergoes repeated public humiliation (mockery, disdain, refusal to answer) that places her in an inferior and socially discredited position in the scene.
